enrichment.summary: Kyle responds to a viewer request about Bill Cosby, stating he fully believes Cosby raped all his accusers and shares his long-held impression of Cosby as dumb, dark, and sociopathic. He recounts specific alleged victim stories (Janice Dickinson, others), argues celebrity wealth shields powerful people from accountability, and connects the case to broader themes of systemic inequality, racism, and the need for statute-of-limitations reform.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Fully believes he raped all accusers; considered him evil and off before the accusations broke
Views him as genuinely dumb — his comedy works because he plays an oblivious viewpoint, not because he's wise
Fame and wealth let powerful people threaten and silence accusers; the system protects stars over victims
Twenty-five women with detailed, corroborated stories crying at a press conference should absolutely be believed
Should be extended — proposes two extra years per victim when 10+ witnesses accuse the same person
Sociopaths who preach loudest against a vice are often the most guilty — Cosby trashing black men's treatment of women is a prime example
Believes she knew and stayed out of fear and financial dependence, likely threatened by Cosby
Believes witnesses clearly showed Brown had his hands up and was shot unjustly; racist system discredited Black witnesses
A world where some earn 600x more than others warps power dynamics and enables abuse
